trajectory_summary: The call builds energy steadily from passive compliance to genuine warmth, driven more by Fidel's personality and Owen's rapport instincts than by deliberate sales architecture. The appointment is booked, the bill is received, and Fidel ends the call enthusiastic. The structural gaps — no bill flip, missing phone-format anchor, misplaced driving check, and a prior-contact disclosure that was never probed — create a closer handoff that is thinner than the energy level suggests.
outcome_dispute: The v1 tag is unknown. Based on the transcript, the outcome is a booked appointment (next Saturday at 8AM) with the bill received during the call. The appointment has a conditional fragility: Owen confirmed a time and date but never anchored the phone format, never stated the one-hour block, and never delivered the 'not driving, at your computer' requirement. Fidel's question at 10:45 ('So you guys can come and look at it') suggests he may expect an in-person visit. The booking is real but structurally incomplete — closer should treat this as a tentative sit requiring confirmation of format.

primary_coaching_focus.quote: So you guys can come and look at it and give me a quote and all that?
primary_coaching_focus.why: Owen booked the appointment without delivering any of the required button_up elements: no phone-format anchor, no one-hour block statement, no computer/not-driving requirement, no 'is that fair?' handshake. Fidel's 'come and look at it' phrasing suggests he may expect an in-person visit. This is the single highest no-show risk on this call — and it's entirely within Owen's control to fix on the next booking. Walk Owen through the button_up sequence as a checklist he runs at the moment of scheduling, not after.

key_moments.quote: I already talked to somebody from your trucking, but go ahead.
key_moments.stewart_read: Fidel discloses prior contact with Ion. Owen acknowledges it ('Oh, you already talked to somebody?') and then immediately pivots to the verify checklist without probing what was discussed, what was promised, or whether a design is already in progress. The schema does not have a scripted protocol for prior-contact disclosure (the excessive_contact_complaint protocol is TBD); this is an unscripted gap. Strategically, probing here could have surfaced a recycled-lead scenario where bill data is already on file — which would have changed the bill-collection path entirely.

key_moments.quote: yeah. I'll just put save money.
key_moments.stewart_read: The verify.interest_in_solar item carries depth_grading:true and expand_to_validate:true — it is explicitly the emotional anchor of the verify section, not a checkbox. Owen self-narrates filling in a field ('I'll just put save money') rather than exploring what saving money means to Fidel, what his bill situation feels like, or what prompted him to fill out the form. Fidel's actual answer ('I just had a guy come knock on the door') is a door-knocker reference that could have opened a scam-anxiety probe or a curiosity-creation moment — Owen walked past both.

key_moments.quote: The first month was 85. My next one is supposed to be 75, so I say $80.
key_moments.stewart_read: Bill captured, not flipped. Owen records the number and moves to roof type. The schema's capture pattern is 'ask + acknowledge + flip' — Owen executed 'ask + move on.' At $80/month Fidel has weak bill pain by residential solar standards, which makes the flip more important, not less: the closer needs an emotional hook to work with. The flip doesn't require a fabricated savings number — it requires redirecting the pain from 'what I pay' to 'what I keep paying forever if nothing changes.'

key_moments.quote: Like, how many watts kilowatts I'm using or something? Pretty much. Yeah. We actually measure that to see how many panels you need. And then we actually have a satellite to look at your roof, and it actually measures how much sun's hitting your roof. So we use both.
key_moments.stewart_read: Owen drifts into technical explanation of the kWh measurement and satellite roof-scan process. This is closer-tier content per setter_scope_discipline. However, the outcome is a genuine curiosity signal ('Interesting.' at 04:29) and a measurable state lift. The strategic hypothesis — that Owen used technical detail deliberately as a curiosity hook — is plausible given the positive result and his self-correction at 08:35. The reactive hypothesis — that Owen drifted because he didn't have the escape hatch ready — is also supported by the fact that he re-enters the same explanation at 08:20 without a new trigger. The coaching implication is not 'don't drift' but 'use the escape hatch to create the same curiosity with less scope exposure.'
